name: Roasted Tomato Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Tomatoes; very red & fresh (about 12-16 medium)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the tomatoes: Cut a cross in the bottom of each tomato with a sharp knife and place them in boiling water for 1 minute. Remove from hot water immediately and plunge into cold water, then slip off their skins and trim over a bowl, catching all the juice.
2. Cut the peeled tomatoes into large chunks or wedges.
3. Peel and chop the onions.
4. Peel and slice the garlic cloves.
5. In a large mixing bowl, toss together the chopped tomatoes (with juice), onions, garlic, olive oil, salt, and basil.
6. Spread the mixture evenly over a baking sheet with edges.
7. Place the baking sheet in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) and roast for about 2 to 2½ hours, stirring once after the first hour and then every 30 minutes or so.
8. Most of the liquid will cook away, and the tomatoes will turn into a soft, thick, slightly caramelized sauce. Serve over pasta, rice, pizza, polenta, in soups, quesadillas, omelets, or alongside any dish that pairs well with tomatoes.
